page.title: Trained reinforcement learning agents across algorithm families and environments.
page.description: Seven agents built through the Hugging Face Deep RL Course, covering tabular Q-learning, policy gradients, DQN, PPO, and Unity ML-Agents.

models.title: PPO · LunarLander-v3
models.summary: Trained with 16 parallel environments and evaluation callbacks to preserve the best checkpoint. Mean reward: 302.83 ± 17.47 across 100 episodes.
models.note: Uses parallel rollout collection and periodic evaluation - closer to how production RL training is structured than a single-environment loop.

models.title: DQN · SpaceInvaders
models.summary: Deep Q-network trained on raw pixel frames from Atari SpaceInvaders, with frame stacking and standard Atari preprocessing.
models.note: Shows how value-based deep RL handles high-dimensional pixel inputs without explicit feature engineering.

models.title: Huggy · Unity ML-Agents
models.summary: PPO agent trained in a Unity environment where the agent learns to run toward a thrown stick using the ML-Agents toolkit.
models.note: Demonstrates how RL extends beyond standard Gym environments into custom simulation setups with continuous action spaces.

models.title: REINFORCE · PixelCopter
models.summary: Policy gradient agent trained on PixelCopter, a continuous state environment where the agent controls altitude to pass through gaps.
models.note: REINFORCE learns directly from full episode returns - a clean implementation of the core policy gradient idea before adding baselines or actor-critic structure.

models.title: REINFORCE · CartPole-v1
models.summary: Policy gradient agent trained to balance a pole on a cart. A clean baseline for verifying a REINFORCE implementation before moving to harder environments.
models.note: CartPole's fast convergence makes it useful as a sanity check - if the policy gradient loop is wrong, it will fail visibly here.

models.title: Q-Learning · Taxi-v3
models.summary: Tabular Q-learning agent trained on the Taxi environment: pick up a passenger and drop them at the correct location in a 5x5 grid.
models.note: Tabular Q-learning stores the full Q-table explicitly, which is tractable for small discrete environments like Taxi but breaks down as state space grows.

models.title: Q-Learning · FrozenLake
models.summary: Tabular Q-learning agent trained on the deterministic (no-slip) version of FrozenLake - navigate from start to goal without falling in a hole.
models.note: The no-slip variant removes environment stochasticity, making it a cleaner case for learning Q-values from a fixed transition model.
